project.name: Module 4: Data Model
project.description: Create lib/data-model.ts — typed, immutable tree traversal and CRUD functions for Hush's bookmark data structure. BookmarkTree is a type alias for Folder (the root node), which has a children array of BookmarkNode[]. All write operations return new trees (immutable). Path addressing uses integer arrays where [0, 2, 1] means root.children[0].children[2].children[1]. Extracted and rewritten from Holy PB's shared.js (~200 lines from 888-line IIFE). Target: ~250 lines.
project.revision_notes: v1.1.0 — Fixes from Claude Code pre-implementation review: (1) CRITICAL: BookmarkTree type mismatch — PRD said { folders: BookmarkNode[] } but types.ts has BookmarkTree = Folder (which has children, not folders). All path references, createEmptyTree, serialization, and test assertions rewritten to match actual types.ts. (2) Added DataModelError with kind discriminant to lib/errors.ts. (3) Added flattenTree to build_from_scratch. (4) Added crypto.randomUUID() clarification note. Implementation note: DataModelError should use context object pattern (DataModelErrorContext interface) matching StorageError/ImportError patterns in errors.ts.
project.parent_plan: Hush Consolidated Build Plan — Day 2 (middle third)

preserve_verbatim: The recursive BookmarkNode tree structure: a tree of Folders containing Bookmarks and sub-Folders. BookmarkTree is the root Folder node. Each Folder has a children: BookmarkNode[] array. This recursive structure is the core of Holy PB's data model.

Path addressing algorithm: integer arrays that index into children arrays at each level. [0] = root.children[0], [0, 2] = root.children[0].children[2] (which must be a Folder), [0, 2, 1] = root.children[0].children[2].children[1]. Empty path [] refers to the root Folder itself.
The JSON serialization format: Holy PB stores the tree as JSON via JSON.stringify/parse. The root is a Folder object with children. Hush preserves this format for backward compatibility.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB's shared.js is an 888-line IIFE mixing tree functions with LRU cache, virtual scroll, debounce, domain history clearing, i18n, and save/load logic — impossible to test or maintain in isolation
falls_short.hush_requires: Pure tree module: lib/data-model.ts contains ONLY tree traversal and CRUD. Zero UI, zero storage, zero caching. ~250 lines max.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB's tree functions return undefined on invalid paths (getItemByPath with bad index) — no error info, callers can't distinguish 'not found' from 'path points to wrong type'
falls_short.hush_requires: All reads return Result<T, DataModelError> with typed error kinds: 'path_not_found', 'invalid_path', 'type_mismatch'. Callers can pattern-match on error.context.kind.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB mutates trees in-place — addBookmark pushes directly to the children array, removeItem splices in-place. No undo capability, risky concurrent access.
falls_short.hush_requires: ALL write operations are immutable — they return a NEW tree. The original tree is never modified. This enables undo (keep previous tree) and safe concurrent reads.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB's findItemPath does linear scan of entire tree on every search — O(n) with no optimization
falls_short.hush_requires: Accept O(n) linear scan for v1.0 (trees are small, typically <1000 items). Document as potential optimization target. Do NOT premature-optimize with index maps.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB does not generate IDs for bookmark/folder items — items have no unique identifier, only their path position (which changes on reorder)
falls_short.hush_requires: Every item gets a unique id via generateId() using crypto.randomUUID(). IDs are stable across reorders. normalizeTree assigns IDs to legacy data without them.

falls_short.deficiency: Holy PB has no type safety — all tree functions work with plain objects, no TypeScript types, no type guards to distinguish Bookmarks from Folders
falls_short.hush_requires: Full TypeScript types from lib/types.ts. Type guards (isBookmark, isFolder) for safe narrowing. All functions have explicit typed signatures.

do_not_incorporate: Holy PB's favicon LRU cache (~100 lines in shared.js) — not related to data model
Holy PB's virtual scroll implementation (~80 lines) — UI concern, Module 12/13
Holy PB's debounce/throttle utilities (~30 lines) — use a standard library if needed
Holy PB's i18n cache — not related to data model
Holy PB's domain history clearing (chrome.history API calls) — Module 9
build_from_scratch: createEmptyTree(): BookmarkTree — returns a root Folder with empty children array, generated id, name 'Root', type 'folder'
getItemByPath(tree: BookmarkTree, path: number[]): Result<BookmarkNode, DataModelError> — traverse children arrays by index
findItemPath(tree: BookmarkTree, id: string): Result<number[], DataModelError> — search tree by id, return path
addBookmark(tree: BookmarkTree, parentPath: number[], bookmark: Omit<Bookmark, 'id'>): Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError> — immutable add with auto-generated id
addFolder(tree: BookmarkTree, parentPath: number[], name: string): Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError> — immutable add folder
removeItem(tree: BookmarkTree, path: number[]): Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError> — immutable remove
moveItem(tree: BookmarkTree, fromPath: number[], toPath: number[], toIndex: number): Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError> — immutable move with cycle detection
updateBookmark(tree: BookmarkTree, path: number[], updates: Partial<Pick<Bookmark, 'title' | 'url'>>): Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError> — immutable update
renameFolder(tree: BookmarkTree, path: number[], newName: string): Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError> — immutable rename
collectAllUrls(tree: BookmarkTree): string[] — for history clearing
countBookmarks(tree: BookmarkTree): { bookmarks: number; folders: number } — tree statistics
flattenTree(tree: BookmarkTree): BookmarkNode[] — flat array of all nodes for search/filter
normalizeTree(tree: BookmarkTree): BookmarkTree — assign IDs to items missing them (legacy Holy PB data import)
isBookmark(node: BookmarkNode): node is Bookmark — type guard
isFolder(node: BookmarkNode): node is Folder — type guard
DataModelError with DataModelErrorContext added to lib/errors.ts (context object pattern matching StorageError/ImportError)

user_stories.title: Core reads: createEmptyTree, getItemByPath, findItemPath
user_stories.description: Create lib/data-model.ts with the three foundational read operations. createEmptyTree returns a root Folder (which IS the BookmarkTree) with empty children. getItemByPath traverses the tree using integer array paths. findItemPath searches by id and returns the path. All return Result<T, DataModelError>.
steps: PREREQUISITE: Add DataModelError to lib/errors.ts using the context object pattern. Create DataModelErrorContext interface with kind: 'path_not_found' | 'invalid_path' | 'type_mismatch' | 'cycle_detected' and optional path: readonly number[]. Create DataModelError class with readonly context: DataModelErrorContext. This matches StorageError and ImportError patterns.

RED: Write tests/unit/lib/data-model.test.ts. Write test: 'createEmptyTree returns a Folder with empty children' — assert result has type: 'folder', children: [], name: 'Root', and a non-empty id string
RED: Write test: 'createEmptyTree result is a valid BookmarkTree' — since BookmarkTree = Folder, assert it satisfies the Folder type (has type, name, children, id, dateAdded)
RED: Write test: 'getItemByPath returns root Folder for empty path []' — the root IS the tree, empty path returns the root itself
RED: Write test: 'getItemByPath returns first child for path [0]' — create tree with one bookmark in root.children, assert path [0] returns that bookmark
RED: Write test: 'getItemByPath returns nested item for path [0, 1]' — root.children[0] is a Folder, its children[1] is a Bookmark, assert correct item returned
RED: Write test: 'getItemByPath returns path_not_found for out-of-bounds index' — path [99] on tree with 1 child, assert DataModelError with context.kind 'path_not_found'
RED: Write test: 'getItemByPath returns type_mismatch when path tries to descend into a Bookmark' — path [0, 0] where children[0] is a Bookmark (not a Folder), assert context.kind 'type_mismatch'
RED: Write test: 'getItemByPath returns invalid_path for negative index' — path [-1], assert context.kind 'invalid_path'
RED: Write test: 'findItemPath finds item by id and returns its path' — create tree with known ids, assert findItemPath returns correct path
RED: Write test: 'findItemPath returns path_not_found for non-existent id'
RED: Write test: 'isBookmark returns true for Bookmark nodes and false for Folder nodes'
RED: Write test: 'isFolder returns true for Folder nodes and false for Bookmark nodes'

GREEN: Create lib/data-model.ts. Import types from '@/lib/types'. Import DataModelError from '@/lib/errors'. Implement createEmptyTree (returns Folder with children: [], name: 'Root', generated id), getItemByPath (walk tree.children by index), findItemPath (recursive DFS by id), isBookmark (checks type === 'bookmark'), isFolder (checks type === 'folder').
REFACTOR: Extract walkPath() helper for index-by-index traversal
acceptance_criteria: createEmptyTree(): BookmarkTree — returns Folder with type: 'folder', name: 'Root', children: [], id: <generated>, dateAdded: <now>
BookmarkTree IS Folder (type alias, not wrapper) — root node accessed directly, not via .folders
getItemByPath(tree, []): returns the root Folder itself
getItemByPath(tree, [0]): returns tree.children[0]
getItemByPath(tree, [0, 2, 1]): returns tree.children[0].children[2].children[1] (intermediate nodes must be Folders)
getItemByPath returns DataModelError with context.kind 'path_not_found' for out-of-bounds
getItemByPath returns DataModelError with context.kind 'type_mismatch' when path descends into Bookmark
getItemByPath returns DataModelError with context.kind 'invalid_path' for negative indices
findItemPath(tree, id): returns number[] path to item with matching id
findItemPath returns DataModelError with context.kind 'path_not_found' for non-existent id
isBookmark and isFolder type guards exported
DataModelError added to lib/errors.ts with DataModelErrorContext interface (context object pattern)
definition_of_done: All 13 read + type guard tests pass
DataModelError added to lib/errors.ts with context object pattern
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/data-model.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Immutable writes: add, remove, update, rename
user_stories.description: Add immutable write operations. Every write returns a NEW BookmarkTree (Folder). The original tree is never modified. All items get auto-generated IDs via generateId(). generateId uses crypto.randomUUID() (on the Crypto interface, not SubtleCrypto — this is correct Web Crypto API usage per CLAUDE.md).
steps: RED: Write test: 'generateId returns a non-empty unique string' — generate 100 ids, assert all unique and non-empty
RED: Write test: 'addBookmark adds bookmark to root children' — addBookmark(emptyTree, [], {title, url, ...}), assert new tree has 1 child that is a Bookmark with generated id
RED: Write test: 'addBookmark adds bookmark to nested folder' — addBookmark(tree, [0], ...) where children[0] is a Folder, assert bookmark added to that folder's children
RED: Write test: 'addBookmark returns type_mismatch if parent path points to Bookmark' — path [0] where children[0] is a Bookmark (can't add children to a Bookmark)
RED: Write test: 'addBookmark does not mutate original tree' — save reference to original children array, call addBookmark, assert original is unchanged (use Object.freeze or reference check)
RED: Write test: 'addFolder adds folder with generated id and empty children'
RED: Write test: 'removeItem removes item at path' — removeItem(tree, [0]), assert tree has one fewer child
RED: Write test: 'removeItem does not mutate original tree'
RED: Write test: 'removeItem returns path_not_found for invalid path'
RED: Write test: 'removeItem cannot remove root (empty path [])' — assert error (cannot remove the tree itself)
RED: Write test: 'updateBookmark updates title and url at path' — updateBookmark(tree, [0], {title: 'new'}), assert title changed
RED: Write test: 'updateBookmark returns type_mismatch if path points to Folder'
RED: Write test: 'updateBookmark does not mutate original tree'
RED: Write test: 'renameFolder renames folder at path'
RED: Write test: 'renameFolder returns type_mismatch if path points to Bookmark'

GREEN: Implement generateId() (crypto.randomUUID()), addBookmark, addFolder, removeItem, updateBookmark, renameFolder. Each creates deep copy of affected path segments. Use structuredClone for deep copy or manual spread at each level.
REFACTOR: Extract immutableSetAtPath() helper for path-based tree updates
acceptance_criteria: generateId(): string — uses crypto.randomUUID(). Note: randomUUID() is on the Crypto interface (global.crypto), not SubtleCrypto. This is correct usage per CLAUDE.md's 'All crypto via Web Crypto API' rule.
All write operations return Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError>
All write operations return NEW trees — original tree is never mutated
addBookmark auto-generates id for new bookmark
addFolder auto-generates id and creates empty children array
removeItem cannot remove root (path [])
updateBookmark only works on Bookmark nodes (context.kind 'type_mismatch' for Folders)
renameFolder only works on Folder nodes (context.kind 'type_mismatch' for Bookmarks)
Deep-freeze test: original tree verified unchanged after each write operation
definition_of_done: Immutability verified via freeze or reference check tests
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/data-model.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: moveItem with cycle detection and reorder
user_stories.description: Add moveItem function: move an item from one path to another, with an explicit destination index. Includes cycle detection to prevent moving a folder into itself or its own descendants.
steps: RED: Write test: 'moveItem moves bookmark from one folder to another' — move tree.children[0].children[0] to tree.children[1] at index 0
RED: Write test: 'moveItem reorders within same folder' — move tree.children[0] to tree.children at index 2 (reorder root children)
RED: Write test: 'moveItem returns cycle_detected when moving folder into itself' — move tree.children[0] into tree.children[0] (parent into itself)
RED: Write test: 'moveItem returns cycle_detected when moving folder into its own descendant' — folder A contains folder B, try to move A into B
RED: Write test: 'moveItem does not mutate original tree'
RED: Write test: 'moveItem returns path_not_found for invalid source path'
RED: Write test: 'moveItem returns path_not_found for invalid destination path'
RED: Write test: 'moveItem cannot move root (path [])' — cannot move the tree itself
GREEN: Implement moveItem(tree, fromPath, toPath, toIndex). Check cycle: toPath must not start with fromPath (would mean moving into self/descendant). Remove from source, insert at destination.
REFACTOR: Extract isDescendantPath(parent, child) helper for cycle detection
acceptance_criteria: moveItem(tree, fromPath, toPath, toIndex): Result<BookmarkTree, DataModelError>
Moves item from fromPath to toPath.children[toIndex]
Supports reorder within same folder (fromPath parent === toPath)
Cycle detection: returns DataModelError with context.kind 'cycle_detected' if toPath starts with fromPath
Cannot move root (path [])
Immutable: returns new tree
Invalid source or destination returns path_not_found
definition_of_done: Cycle detection verified for direct and nested cases
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/data-model.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Utilities: collectAllUrls, countBookmarks, flattenTree
user_stories.description: Add read-only utility functions for tree analysis. collectAllUrls extracts all bookmark URLs (needed for domain history clearing in Module 9). countBookmarks returns tree statistics. flattenTree returns a flat array of all nodes (needed for search in Module 17).
steps: RED: Write test: 'collectAllUrls returns all bookmark URLs in tree' — tree with 5 bookmarks across nested folders, assert 5 URLs returned
RED: Write test: 'collectAllUrls returns empty array for empty tree'
RED: Write test: 'collectAllUrls does not include folder names (only bookmark URLs)'

RED: Write test: 'countBookmarks returns correct counts' — tree with 3 bookmarks and 2 folders, assert { bookmarks: 3, folders: 2 }. Note: root folder is NOT counted (it's the container, not user-created).
RED: Write test: 'countBookmarks returns zeros for empty tree'
RED: Write test: 'flattenTree returns flat array of all nodes' — tree with nested structure, assert flat array length matches total items
RED: Write test: 'flattenTree preserves node references (same objects)' — since it's read-only, nodes in flat array should be the same references as in tree
GREEN: Implement collectAllUrls (recursive, collect node.url where isBookmark), countBookmarks (recursive count), flattenTree (recursive flatten of all children).
REFACTOR: Extract a general treeWalk/treeReduce helper if pattern repeats across all three
acceptance_criteria: collectAllUrls(tree): string[] — all bookmark URLs, no folder names
countBookmarks(tree): { bookmarks: number; folders: number } — root folder NOT counted
flattenTree(tree): BookmarkNode[] — flat array for search/filter
All three work on empty tree (return empty/zeros)
All three handle deeply nested structures

user_stories.title: JSON serialization compatibility and normalizeTree
user_stories.description: Verify JSON roundtrip compatibility with Holy PB's data format. The serialized format is a Folder object (the root) with children, name, type, id, dateAdded. normalizeTree assigns IDs to items imported from Holy PB that don't have them. Test with a 1000-bookmark tree for performance.
steps: RED: Write test: 'JSON.stringify(tree) produces valid JSON that JSON.parse recovers' — create tree, stringify, parse, deep-equal
RED: Write test: 'serialized tree root is a Folder object with children array' — parse JSON, assert root has type: 'folder', children: Array
RED: Write test: 'serialized bookmarks have type, title, url, id, dateAdded' — assert all Bookmark fields present
RED: Write test: 'serialized folders have type, name, children, id, dateAdded' — assert all Folder fields present
RED: Write test: 'normalizeTree assigns IDs to items without id field' — create tree with items missing id, assert all have ids after normalize
RED: Write test: 'normalizeTree preserves existing IDs' — items that already have IDs keep them
RED: Write test: 'normalizeTree handles deeply nested tree' — 10 levels deep, all get IDs
RED: Write test: '1000-bookmark tree roundtrip completes in under 100ms' — generate large tree, JSON roundtrip, assert performance
GREEN: Implement normalizeTree (recursive, assign generateId() where id is missing or empty). JSON serialization is automatic via JSON.stringify since BookmarkTree is plain objects.
REFACTOR: Ensure normalizeTree returns a new tree (immutable)
acceptance_criteria: BookmarkTree serializes as a Folder object: { type: 'folder', name: string, children: BookmarkNode[], id: string, dateAdded: number }
JSON roundtrip (stringify then parse) preserves complete tree structure
normalizeTree(tree): BookmarkTree — assigns IDs to items missing them (legacy import)
normalizeTree preserves existing IDs
normalizeTree is immutable (returns new tree)
1000-bookmark tree roundtrip completes in under 100ms

what_exists.project_scaffold: Module 1 complete. Full CI, testing infrastructure, Sentry, shadcn/ui. 203 unit tests, 10 E2E tests.
what_exists.crypto_module: Module 2 complete. lib/crypto.ts with encrypt/decrypt/CRYPTO_CONFIG. 266+ tests.
what_exists.types: lib/types.ts defines: Bookmark { type: 'bookmark'; title: string; url: string; id: string; dateAdded: number }, Folder { type: 'folder'; name: string; children: BookmarkNode[]; id: string; dateAdded: number }, BookmarkNode = Bookmark | Folder, BookmarkTree = Folder (TYPE ALIAS — the root node IS a Folder, accessed directly via tree.children, NOT tree.folders), EncryptedStore, Result<T, E>
what_exists.errors: lib/errors.ts defines: StorageError (with StorageErrorContext { key, operation, reason? }), InvalidPasswordError. NOTE: DataModelError does NOT exist yet — DATAMODEL-001 must add it using context object pattern.

what_does_not_exist.data_model: lib/data-model.ts does not exist. No tree functions exist yet.
what_does_not_exist.data_model_error: DataModelError does NOT exist in lib/errors.ts. DATAMODEL-001 must add it with DataModelErrorContext interface (context object pattern matching StorageError/ImportError).

starting_state.type_system_critical_note: BookmarkTree = Folder. This means: (1) The root of the tree IS a Folder with children, name, id, type, dateAdded. (2) There is NO { folders: BookmarkNode[] } wrapper. (3) createEmptyTree returns a Folder. (4) getItemByPath([]) returns the root Folder itself. (5) All path navigation uses .children, never .folders. (6) JSON serialization produces a Folder object at the root level.

relevant_files.what_to_extract: Tree traversal and CRUD functions (~200 lines scattered through IIFE). Key functions: getItemByPath (path array traversal), findItemByPath (search by matching), addBookmark, removeItem. Search for these function names.
relevant_files.extraction_notes: Extract algorithms, rewrite with TypeScript types, Result returns, and immutability. The Holy PB versions mutate in-place and return undefined on error.

tech_stack.id_generation: crypto.randomUUID() — this is on the Crypto interface (global.crypto.randomUUID()), NOT on SubtleCrypto. It's part of the Web Crypto API but doesn't require crypto.subtle. Available in all modern browsers and Node.js 19+. This is correct usage per CLAUDE.md's 'All crypto via Web Crypto API' rule.
tech_stack.testing: Vitest with jsdom. crypto.randomUUID() is available in Node.js test environment. No mocking needed for ID generation.
forbidden_patterns: Mutating tree in-place — all writes return new trees
Returning undefined for errors — use Result<T, DataModelError>
React or DOM imports — lib/ module purity rule
browser.* or chrome.* imports — data model is pure logic
External tree manipulation libraries (immer, immutable.js) — manual immutability with structuredClone or spread

path_addressing.description: Paths are integer arrays indexing into children arrays at each tree level.

examples.[0, 2]: tree.children[0].children[2] — third child of first folder (tree.children[0] must be a Folder)
examples.[0, 2, 1]: tree.children[0].children[2].children[1] — second child of that nested folder

rules: Each integer indexes into .children array of the Folder at that level
Intermediate path segments MUST be Folders (type_mismatch error if Bookmark)

immutability_pattern.description: Every write operation clones the path from root to the modified node. Only the affected branch is cloned; sibling branches share references with the original tree.
immutability_pattern.implementation: Use structuredClone() for deep copy of affected nodes, or manual spread: { ...folder, children: [...folder.children] } at each level in the path. structuredClone is simpler but slightly slower — acceptable for trees under 10K items.

licensing.data_model_attribution: Tree traversal and path addressing algorithms derived from Holy Private Bookmarks shared.js (GPL-3.0). Immutability, type safety, Result returns, and ID generation are new.

read_first: lib/types.ts — CRITICAL: BookmarkTree = Folder. Read the actual type definitions.
lib/errors.ts (StorageError pattern — DataModelError should follow same context object structure)
/tmp/holy-pb-reference/src/js/shared.js (tree functions — search for getItemByPath, addBookmark, removeItem)

inputs.what_it_provides: Bookmark, Folder, BookmarkNode, BookmarkTree (= Folder), Result<T, E>
inputs.how_to_use: import type { Bookmark, Folder, BookmarkNode, BookmarkTree, Result } from '@/lib/types';

inputs.what_it_provides: Base error classes. DataModelError must be ADDED here using context object pattern.
inputs.modification_needed: Add: DataModelErrorContext interface { readonly kind: 'path_not_found' | 'invalid_path' | 'type_mismatch' | 'cycle_detected'; readonly path?: readonly number[] } and DataModelError class with readonly context: DataModelErrorContext (matching StorageError pattern).

outputs.consumed_by: Module 5 (Bookmark Import — generateId, normalizeTree), Module 8 (Background — tree operations), Module 10-13 (UI — all CRUD), Module 15a (Sync — tree merge)
outputs.description: Data model module. ~250 lines. Exports: createEmptyTree, getItemByPath, findItemPath, addBookmark, addFolder, removeItem, moveItem, updateBookmark, renameFolder, collectAllUrls, countBookmarks, flattenTree, generateId, normalizeTree, isBookmark, isFolder.

outputs.consumed_by: All modules that handle data model errors
outputs.description: DataModelError + DataModelErrorContext added with context object pattern.

known_risks.issue: structuredClone performance on large trees — cloning a 1000-item tree on every operation could be slow
known_risks.action: Path-based cloning (only clone affected branch) is preferred over full tree clone. Test with 1000-bookmark tree. If too slow, switch to manual spread at each path level.

known_risks.issue: crypto.randomUUID() availability in test environment — may not be available in older Node.js or some jsdom versions
known_risks.action: Node.js 20 LTS has crypto.randomUUID(). If test env lacks it, provide a simple fallback using crypto.getRandomValues().

known_risks.issue: Cycle detection edge cases in moveItem — comparing path prefixes has subtleties (e.g., [0, 1] is NOT a prefix of [0, 10])
known_risks.action: isDescendantPath must compare element-by-element, not string prefix. Test with similar-looking but different paths.

known_risks.issue: normalizeTree called on data with partial IDs — some items have IDs, some don't
known_risks.action: normalizeTree only assigns IDs where id is missing, undefined, or empty string. Existing IDs preserved.

format_rules.format:
Append-only. Each entry: ## DATAMODEL-XXX — <title>
- Status: PASS | FAIL (attempt N)
- Tests: N passing, N failing
- Coverage: N% branches
- Notes: <any issues encountered>
- Timestamp: ISO 8601
format_rules.update_trigger: After each story completion or failure

metadata.session_protocol: One Claude Code session. Add DataModelError (with DataModelErrorContext) to errors.ts first. Then generate data-model.ts + data-model.test.ts. Run tsc + eslint + vitest. Commit.
metadata.quality_gate: All data model tests pass. tsc clean. No mutations (immutability tests). lib/ coverage >= 80%. Line count <= 300. Zero browser API calls (crypto.randomUUID is allowed).
metadata.human_review_required: Verify all path references use .children (never .folders). Verify immutability tests actually detect mutation. Review cycle detection in moveItem.

execution_rules.execution_order: DATAMODEL-001 first (includes errors.ts DataModelError prerequisite), then 002, then 003, then 004 and 005 (parallel), then 006 as final validation.
execution_rules.clarifying_questions: You ARE encouraged to ask clarifying questions about intent or trade-offs. The PRD should contain all factual context needed.
execution_rules.progress_logging: Update prd.json + progress.md per progress_log.format_rules after each story.
execution_rules.commit_protocol: Commit after each story. Format: 'feat(data-model): DATAMODEL-XXX — <title>'.
execution_rules.failure_protocol: Log failure in progress.md with root cause. Increment attempt_count. Max 3 attempts.
